  "findings": [
    "Reversed polarity at receptacle: hot and neutral conductors are swapped."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize circuit, verify with voltage tester, and reconnect conductors to correct terminals (brass to hot, silver to neutral).",
    "Re-inspect all receptacles on the same circuit for similar miswiring."
  ],
  "rationale": "Reversed polarity creates a shock hazard because the neutral slot becomes energized. NEC 406.4 requires receptacles to be wired with correct polarity. This is a non-compliant and defective condition that must be corrected before close-out.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 406.4"
  ]
}
